TextENGNew: ``What?@k@vS06/06/150601133. What did you just say?``
``This is from a reliable source, but it seems they have identified the person who murdered Satoshi-kun's aunt.``
Several weeks had passed since the aunt's murder.
Satoshi-kun's whereabouts remained completely unknown, of course.
...No new information, no progress of any kind.
It was out of nowhere that Kasai suddenly told me.
``Does that mean the police arrested someone?``
``From what I hear, it was not so much an arrest -- a man being interrogated for a separate crime confessed to it as an additional offense.``
changeReason: Original omits how the confession arose; new captures the JPN distinction: not an arrest but a confession during interrogation for a separate crime.

TextENGNew: ...Something about Kasai's wording felt off.
If the culprit had been Satoshi-kun himself, Kasai would not have phrased it that way.
It sounded as though someone other than Satoshi-kun was the culprit.
changeReason: Original implies Satoshi-kun's innocence ('wasn't the culprit'); new implies a different perpetrator exists ('someone other than Satoshi-kun was the culprit').

TextENGNew: ``...Kasai.@k@vS06/06/150601137. In the end, who was the culprit...?``
It was not Satoshi-kun...?@k Those words nearly slipped out, but I caught them in my throat.
I had been certain it was Satoshi-kun.
No -- the police, and Ooishi too, must have thought the same.
The fact that Ooishi had tried to provoke me meant he himself was convinced Satoshi-kun was the culprit.
``Rest assured.@k@vS06/15/151500187. It does not appear to be Satoshi-kun.@k@vS06/15/151500188. It seems it was the work of a deranged individual... imitating the serial mysterious deaths.``
changeReason: Original says 'aiming to continue the series'; new says 'imitating the serial mysterious deaths' -- continuing vs imitating are meaningfully different.

TextENGNew: ``What?? ...A deranged individual?@k@vS06/06/150601139. What does that mean?``
Kasai shrugged.

``I am afraid that is all I know.@k@vS06/15/151500190. ...Apparently, this case has been designated as a confidential investigation.@k@vS06/15/151500191. So almost no information is being circulated.``
changeReason: Original abbreviates to 'I don't know'; new captures full JPN content about the confidential investigation designation.

TextENGNew: ``A confidential investigation?@k@vS06/06/150601141. What is that?``
changeReason: Original asks 'Why?' (motivation); new asks 'What is that?' (definition) -- different questions entirely.

TextENGNew: ``It seems to be a countermeasure against reputational damage to the village from the serial mysterious deaths.@k@vS06/15/151500193. In kidnapping cases, the news is kept out of the papers while negotiations with the culprit are ongoing, right?@k@vS06/15/151500194. It appears to be something along those lines.``
changeReason: New adds significant detail from JPN about kidnapping case suppression analogy and reputational damage context.

TextENGNew: Come to think of it... I had not seen anything about the aunt's murder on TV or in the newspapers.

``After all, incidents have occurred on the day of Watanagashi for four consecutive years now.@k@vS06/15/151500196. Apparently there are quite a few people making a fuss about it being Oyashiro-sama's curse and finding it entertaining.``
changeReason: Original truncates; new includes complete JPN content about people finding Oyashiro-sama's curse 'entertaining.'

TextENGNew: ``Oh yes, about that deranged individual who killed the aunt -- he apparently confessed that he did it because he wanted to imitate the serial mysterious deaths.``
Since Kasai said he knew nothing beyond that, I stopped pressing him and turned the questions inward.
...Honestly, this was unexpected.
I had been convinced Satoshi-kun was the killer.
...That was precisely why I had believed he had no choice but to disappear.
But... unbelievably, the aunt's killer turned out to be some nobody -- a complete stranger.
changeReason: Original says criminal 'had no relation to the aunt'; new correctly renders JPN どこの馬の骨とも知れぬ輩 as 'some nobody -- a complete stranger.'

TextENGNew: Then... Satoshi-kun had nothing to do with the murder?
If so... if he was truly unrelated...
Then why did he have to disappear...?
changeReason: New adds 'have to' (compulsion matching JPN しなければならない) absent in original.

TextENGNew: Honestly... I could not bring myself to accept this so-called real culprit who had popped up out of nowhere.
And the phrase Kasai had used -- 'the fourth year of the serial mysterious deaths' -- also struck me as wrong.
changeReason: Different phrase attributed to Kasai: 'four years in a row now' vs 'the fourth year of the serial mysterious deaths.'

TextENGNew: I had always believed this year's incident was a sad, isolated act born of Satoshi-kun being driven to desperation,@k ...entirely unrelated to the serial mysterious deaths of previous years.
changeReason: New adds the critical concept of it being 'a sad, isolated act' (JPN 単独のもの) which is key to Shion's reasoning.

TextENGNew: A nagging dissonance -- as if Satoshi-kun's case was... no longer Satoshi-kun's case.
The Hinamizawa Village Serial Mysterious Deaths are popularly known as 'Oyashiro-sama's Curse.'
Satoshi-kun's case had been absorbed into the serial mysterious deaths.
...In other words, Satoshi-kun's case had been absorbed into 'Oyashiro-sama's Curse.'
What an unpleasant way to put it.
Because taken to its logical end, it could be read as: 'Satoshi-kun was swallowed by Oyashiro-sama's Curse.'
changeReason: New shows the reasoning process and quotes the condensed conclusion, matching JPN structure; original simply states the outcome.

TextENGNew: ``Yes, unfortunately.@k@vS06/11/150700255. The report about someone resembling him boarding a Tokyo-bound bullet train remains unconfirmed as well.@k@vS06/11/150700256. Honestly, we do not even know whether he is inside or outside the prefecture.``
changeReason: New specifies 'someone resembling him' and 'bullet train' (新幹線) where original said only 'Satoshi-kun took a train to Tokyo.'

TextENGNew: Ooishi said this while peeling the inner seal off a new jar of instant coffee.
changeReason: Original says 'can of instant coffee'; new correctly says 'jar' -- JPN 瓶 means bottle/jar.

TextENGNew: ``You were so confident, so insistent that Satoshi-kun was the culprit -- I assumed you had already tracked him down.``
``Na ha ha ha ha.@k@vS06/11/150700258. No, no -- it is quite embarrassing, really.``
With a dry laugh, Ooishi set a cup of coffee brewed with far-too-hot water in front of me.
``...You certainly have sharp ears, though.``
``Pardon?``

``This is supposed to be a classified investigation, yet someone is leaking things left and right.@k@vS06/11/150700262. ...Good grief.@k@vS06/11/150700263. Our counterintelligence could use some work.``
changeReason: Original focuses on Shion having info; new focuses on systematic leaks, matching JPN perspective.

TextENGNew: Ooishi grinned knowingly.
He seemed to have already guessed why I had come here.
``Well, tarnished or not, I am still a Sonozaki.@k@vS06/06/150601145. I can pick up a rumor here and there.``
changeReason: New preserves self-deprecating idiom 'tarnished or not' from JPN 腐っても, absent in original.

TextENGNew: I returned a knowing smile of my own.@k ...In games like these, whoever bluffs best wins.
``Well, why not.@k@vS06/11/150700265. We have been pretty candid with each other, after all.@k@vS06/11/150700266. Sure, let us have a chat.@k@vS06/11/150700267. Nfu fu fu.``
``Let me ask first.@k@vS06/06/150601147. Who on earth is this real culprit?@k@vS06/06/150601148. I heard he was some kind of deranged individual -- what does that even mean?``

``Na ha ha ha!@k@vS06/11/150700269. 'What does that mean,' she asks...@k@vS06/11/150700270. Believe me, I would like to know too.@k@vS06/11/150700271. ...I had bet everything on Satoshi-kun being the culprit.@k@vS06/11/150700272. Quite the dark horse to come galloping out of nowhere.``
changeReason: New preserves JPN gambling metaphor 万馬券 as 'dark horse'; original uses generic 'surprising.'

TextENGNew: Ooishi dropped heavily onto the sofa, laced his fingers behind his head, and continued with a wry smile while gazing up at the ceiling.
``......The other day, I suddenly received a call from the prefectural police.@k@vS06/11/150700274. They said a man already in custody had confessed to the housewife's murder.``
``Who is he?``

``A real piece of work who got into drugs.@k@vS06/11/150700276. He has been in and out of prison, over and over.@k@vS06/11/150700277. He said the Hinamizawa serial mysterious deaths seemed interesting, so he wanted to carry out the fourth year's curse himself... or something to that effect.``
``...Are you certain he is the real culprit?``

``I did not question him personally, but...@k@vS06/11/150700279. ...the statement the prefectural police took from him was packed with what we call 'information only the culprit could know.'@k@vS06/11/150700280. He says he disposed of the murder weapon somewhere in the Ouji River.``
``They are dredging the river as we speak.@k@vS06/11/150700282. If the weapon turns up where he said it was, that will seal it.``
``...Even if the weapon is not found, would the case be essentially closed?``

``Most likely.@k@vS06/11/150700284. His statement accurately describes the condition of the crime scene and the victim.@k@vS06/11/150700285. Down to fine details that only the actual assailant would know.``
``...........You do not seem entirely convinced, though.``
Ooishi crossed his legs while still looking up at the ceiling and fell silent for a long moment.
``The thing is, that utter fool...@k@vS06/11/150700288. he is already dead.@k@vS06/11/150700289. By the time we received the call, he had already passed away.``
``Dead?``

``...You know those sporks they give with school lunches?@k@vS06/11/150700291. They serve those with meals in the holding cells too.@k@vS06/11/150700292. He swallowed the handle of one and choked to death.@k@vS06/11/150700293. ...Whether it was suicide or some kind of delirium, it is hard to say.``
changeReason: New specifies 'swallowed the handle' and 'choked to death'; original is less specific.

TextENGNew: Imagining someone swallowing a spoon, I felt my own throat constrict. I gagged slightly.

``So the investigation could not be pursued to full satisfaction.@k@vS06/11/150700295. ...I am not satisfied with it myself, but... the higher-ups seem perfectly content.@k@vS06/11/150700296. It seems the case is heading toward being closed with that copycat as the culprit.``
changeReason: New provides significantly more detail from JPN about Ooishi's dissatisfaction and case trajectory.

TextENGNew: Ooishi shifted his gaze from the ceiling back to me.
His expression had turned just slightly serious.
``...This is strictly my personal opinion, all right?@k@vS06/11/150700298. Keep it between us.``
``......I believe that utter fool is some kind of mistake.``
``Either a colossal misunderstanding caused by some chain of coincidences, or perhaps............... you know?``
``Na ha ha ha ha.``
``I have absolutely no idea what you are getting at, Ooishi-ojisama.``
``I still believe the real culprit is Houjou Satoshi.``
``......You say that with such conviction.``

``Unfortunately, the crime scene investigation did not turn up a single trace that could identify the culprit.@k@vS06/11/150700304. After Satoshi-kun disappeared, we obtained a warrant to search his home.@k@vS06/11/150700305. We went through the entire house, but still came up with nothing.``
``...And yet you still suspect Satoshi-kun is the culprit?``

``...How you can brand someone a murderer so definitively without a shred of evidence is beyond me.@k@vS06/06/150601159. Truly impressive.@k@vS06/06/150601160. Is that what they call years of experience?``
``Indeed.@k@vS06/11/150700308. Call it the gut instinct of a man who has made his living in this trade for decades.``
Ooishi smiled with self-satisfaction, but I found nothing reassuring about it.
``......Where did Satoshi-kun go...?``

``One of the key reasons I was so convinced Satoshi-kun was the culprit was precisely because he disappeared.@k@vS06/11/150700310. If he is not the culprit... that reasoning loses most of its weight.``
changeReason: Original presents disappearance as full proof; new correctly says 'one of the key reasons,' matching JPN ひとつこそが.

TextENGNew: ``......What if the aunt's killer really was that copycat, and it had nothing to do with Satoshi-kun's disappearance?@k@vS06/06/150601163. ...What if Satoshi-kun's disappearance was the Sonozaki main family's doing, to tear us apart?@k@vS06/06/150601164. Completely unrelated to the murder... is that a possible reading...?``
Was Satoshi-kun a perpetrator...@k ...or a victim?
...The reason for his disappearance grew murkier and murkier, fading into haze...
``Hmmmmmm.........@k@vS06/11/150700312. ...I do not think they would go so far as to make someone vanish just over a romance with a rival family.``
``......What is the view from inside the Sonozaki family, then?@k@vS06/11/150700314. ...Do you know anything about that, Shion-san?``
``If I did, I would not be here.``
``Na ha ha ha. Fair point, fair point.``
Ooishi scratched his head and laughed broadly.@k ...Once the laughter died down, he leaned forward.
``All right then, how about this?@k@vS06/11/150700317. Why don't we form an agreement?@k@vS06/11/150700318. A gentlemen's agreement.``
``An agreement?@k@vS06/06/150601167. About what?``
``An intelligence-sharing agreement on the Satoshi-kun disappearance case.``

``...If you come across any information about Satoshi-kun's disappearance through underground channels, please share it with me.@k@vS06/11/150700321. Naturally, I will also provide you with any information I obtain about Satoshi-kun.@k@vS06/11/150700322. ...What do you say?``
changeReason: New adds 'through underground channels' (裏の界隈), absent in original.

TextENGNew: ``That sounds awfully one-sided and convenient for you.``
Ooishi gave a wry laugh.

``...Shion-san.@k@vS06/11/150700324. ......At this rate, the housewife's murder will be pinned on that dead fool and the case closed.@k@vS06/11/150700325. When that happens, Satoshi-kun's status will be reduced to that of a mere runaway.``
changeReason: New emphasizes the suspect being dead ('that dead fool'), a key plot point.

TextENGNew: ``In other words, any active investigation will be called off.``
changeReason: Original says investigation will be 'less active'; new says it will be 'called off' -- meaningfully different outcomes.

TextENGNew: ``But I alone will continue.@k@vS06/11/150700328. I alone will keep searching for Satoshi-kun.``
``...You and I are both searching for Satoshi-kun's whereabouts -- I believe we are aligned in that regard, are we not?``
``Our motives could not be more different, though.@k@vS06/06/150601171. I simply want to confirm he is safe. You want to arrest him.``
``Nfu fu fu...@k@vS06/11/150700331. Once the murder case is settled, Satoshi-kun will effectively be innocent, you know.@k@vS06/11/150700332. Whatever the truth may actually be.``
``...Somehow I doubt that.``
``Na ha ha ha ha...``
Ooishi laughed his vulgar laugh again.@k But the look in his eyes was anything but amused.
``......Well, let us get along from here on.@k@vS06/11/150700336. They do say the enemy of your enemy is your friend.``
``I am not exactly at odds with the Sonozaki main family, you know.``
``Oh, really?@k@vS06/11/150700338. Well then, my apologies.@k@vS06/11/150700339. Nfu fu fu...``
Ooishi Kuraudo, huh...
...I could see why Sis and Kasai had warned me -- he was someone you would not want as an enemy, but you could never let your guard down around him as an ally, either.
Quite the wily old fox, not easily dealt with...
changeReason: Original uses 'weasel'; new uses 'fox' -- JPN タヌキ idiomatically means crafty person; different animal metaphor connotations.

TextENGNew: I was not thirsty, but I took a sip of coffee to fill the pause.
...Outside the window, the ordinary world I inhabited every day stretched out as usual.
But... Satoshi-kun alone was missing from that ordinary world.
Absent from the ordinary, as naturally as if it were meant to be.
...And someday, I was afraid, his absence itself would become ordinary.
......Where was Satoshi-kun... what was he doing?
``..............I wonder what Satoshi-kun is doing right now...``
``..........I hope he is safe.``
``Tch.@k@vS06/06/150601175. ......You think there is little chance he is alive, yet you say that.@k@vS06/06/150601176. Do not try to comfort me...``
``Do you know what they are calling his disappearance in Hinamizawa?``
``What?``
``...They say he was 'spirited away by the demon'...``
changeReason: New uses 'spirited away by the demon' for 鬼隠し instead of non-standard 'demoned away,' significantly clearer.

TextENGNew: 'Onikakushi' -- spirited away by the demon -- means the same as 'kamikakushi,' being spirited away.@k It is a local dialect particular to this region.
changeReason: New preserves Japanese terms 'Onikakushi' and 'kamikakushi' which are important recurring plot terminology.

TextENGNew: ``......Snatched away to the demon's realm at the bottom of the Onigafuchi Swamp, you mean...?@k@vS06/06/150601179. ......How ridiculous...``
changeReason: Original says 'taken to Hell'; new correctly says 'the demon's realm' -- JPN 鬼の国 is not Hell.

TextENGNew: ``Be that as it may, the serial mysterious deaths have now reached their fourth year.``
``...It is premature, but people are already whispering about whether it will happen again next year.``
``Are you saying the aunt's murder was the work of Oyashiro-sama's curse?``

``...Looking at the big picture, the victim was a member of the Houjou family.@k@vS06/11/150700346. She was not entirely unconnected to those branded as war criminals during the dam conflict.@k@vS06/11/150700347. And with it happening on the day of Watanagashi yet again, it is hardly surprising that people are having a field day with it.``
``..........How ridiculous. Oyashiro-sama's curse, indeed.``
Even as I said that... I could not quite shake the ominous feeling from my mind.
Why had Satoshi-kun vanished...?
Was it the work of the Sonozaki main family, punishing him for our romance?
changeReason: New includes the specific motivation 'punishing him for our romance' from JPN 恋仲を咎めた, omitted in original.

TextENGNew: Had the police pressure driven him to flee?
In the first place, was Satoshi-kun even the one who killed his aunt?
And so -- why did Satoshi-kun disappear?
Satoshi-kun was Satoshi-kun.@k Oyashiro-sama's curse had nothing to do with it.
changeReason: 'He's on his own' vs 'Satoshi-kun was Satoshi-kun' convey similar independence; both followed by dismissal of curse.

TextENGNew: But... ...hmm?@k An unpleasant sense of dissonance.
Satoshi-kun and Oyashiro-sama's curse.
......I felt as though someone had once mentioned those two things together.
...Wait, when had I heard something like that...?
That was it.@k ...That rainy day.
Sheltering from the rain in the bus stop shed... Ryuuguu Rena had said it.
changeReason: Original says 'shack'; new says 'bus stop shed' -- JPN 停留所の小屋 specifically means bus stop shelter.

TextENGNew: In that moment... the eerie story Rena had told me came flooding back.
``Somewhere in his heart, Satoshi-kun...@k@vS06/02/150200065. ...wants to abandon Hinamizawa and escape far away.``
That was what Ryuuguu Rena had said.
Yes -- Rena had clearly stated, even at that early point, that Satoshi-kun wanted to abandon Hinamizawa and flee.
And... right, she had also said this.
``Everything Satoshi-kun is experiencing... is a harbinger of Oyashiro-sama's curse.``
She said it.@k She definitely said it.
She had clearly spoken the words: Oyashiro-sama's curse.
The Hinamizawa Village Serial Mysterious Deaths -- four years running.
...Had Satoshi-kun been... manipulated by Oyashiro-sama's curse... led to kill his aunt, and then 'spirited away by the demon'...?
changeReason: New uses 'manipulated/led to kill' and 'spirited away by the demon' vs original's 'controlled' and 'demoned away.'

TextENGNew: ..........That was absurd.@k There was no such thing as a curse.
The image of Satoshi-kun's smile in my heart grew more and more distorted, fading away... disappearing......
...The air conditioning felt too harsh, so I moved to a different seat.
My university notebook, a pencil case jangling with keychains, and a paper cup of milk tea with the milk floating on top in a thin film.
I gathered everything up and moved seats.
Once I sat down, I spread my notebook open again and returned to the world of deep contemplation, writing out my thoughts.
The Hinamizawa Village Serial Mysterious Deaths.
Popularly known as 'Oyashiro-sama's Curse.'

For most people, myself included, who had only ever viewed each year's incident individually,@k even though they were 'serial incidents,' the perception was always of separate, discrete events lined up -- strangely, the notion that they formed 'one grand incident' remained faint.
changeReason: New captures JPN nuance about how 'strangely' the incidents weren't recognized as one grand event, context simplified in original.

TextENGNew: But when I wrote out the past incidents like this, it became clear that all of these connected events were driven by a single underlying will.
I wrote out the victims.
Year one: the construction site manager.@k　　　@rYear two: Satoshi-kun's parents.@k@rYear three: the head priest.@k　　　@rYear four: Satoshi-kun's aunt.
......For the fourth year alone, Satoshi-kun himself might be more aptly called the victim than his aunt.
When I traced through these cases along the thread of lingering resentment from the dam conflict, they fell into a clear pattern.
First, year one.
The construction site manager was killed because his very existence was the most visible symbol of the enemy during the dam conflict.
The true enemy during the dam war was, of course, the Ministry of Construction and the government, but those were abstract concepts -- too vague to serve as a concrete image of 'the enemy.'
The site manager, on the other hand, who had openly bared his hostility and hurled abuse at the villagers, was the easiest figure to picture as the village's enemy.
...If Oyashiro-sama were truly to deliver a curse in the truest sense, it should have been against the heads of the Ministry of Construction or the government.
The fact that the curse fell on the 'enemy' the villagers could most easily picture was itself a kind of proof that the village's collective will had influenced the selection of the target.
Next, year two.
The accident that killed Satoshi-kun's parents.
...They were said to have fallen from a railing at a scenic overlook, but whether it was truly an accident was dubious.
If the site manager represented the 'enemy,' then Satoshi-kun's parents -- villagers who had sided with the dam -- were positioned as 'traitors.'
During the dam conflict, the Houjou family was relentlessly attacked and made an example of, in order to deter others from supporting the dam.
Thus, during the dam war, they were 'necessary' -- they served an important role as the designated 'traitors' for propaganda purposes.
And that was precisely why...
...once the dam conflict ended, they had outlived their usefulness.
The site manager, symbol of the external enemy.@k The Houjou couple, symbol of the internal enemy.
With these two targets struck down by the 'curse' over the course of two years, it seemed as though the grudges from the dam war had been settled.
But in reality, the settling of accounts continued for one more year.
And so, year three.
The head priest of the Furude family, who was also the leader of one of the Three Families, suddenly died of illness.
His wife reportedly drowned herself that same night.
She was said to have left a note declaring she was offering her own life to appease Oyashiro-sama's anger.
Of course, whether it was truly illness or truly suicide, suspicion lingered.
Mion herself had previously admitted that the Sonozaki family did not look favorably on the head priest.
During the time when the entire village should have been united against the dam, the priest had taken an opportunistic, fence-sitting stance, which had infuriated the hawks.
He may not have been a traitor outright, but he was certainly uncooperative.
Viewed this way, I noticed that from year one onward -- 'enemy,'@k 'traitor,'@k 'non-cooperator'@k -- the 'curse' had been carried out in descending order of hostility.
Yes -- the 'curse' was, in essence, 'payment.'
It was nothing other than retribution against the war criminals of the dam conflict.
...And then, year four.
The fourth year's incident was... honestly, difficult to parse.
At first, I had believed this incident, while it fell in the fourth year, was a standalone act.
I had thought it was an entirely separate case, unrelated to the serial incidents of the previous three years.
But when I wrote out the aunt's murder objectively like this...
...The victim was a 'relative of a traitor.'
After 'enemy,' 'traitor,' and 'non-cooperator,' adding 'relative of a traitor' to the lineage of victims did not feel out of place.
As a fourth rank in the hierarchy, it seemed apt.
On the very day of the festival honoring Oyashiro-sama, the village's guardian deity, the war criminals of the dam conflict were being 'struck down by the curse,' year by year.
Yes -- just as I had been made to 'pay' at the Sonozaki main house, their sins were being settled.
...Writing it all out like this, even the fourth year's incident began to look like it was part of the serial mysterious deaths.
Whether the culprit was Satoshi-kun or the suspect who died after confessing...@k ...perhaps they were nothing more than 'executors' built into a four-year-long scenario.

Even if Satoshi-kun was the culprit, and even if his motive was sympathetic and rooted in his family situation...@k ...the result had been neatly incorporated into the 'Hinamizawa Village Serial Mysterious Deaths' -- the settling of accounts from the dam war.
...The incident Satoshi-kun had carried out in tragic resolve.
changeReason: Original says 'the result of his pain'; new says 'carried out in tragic resolve' -- JPN 悲壮な決意 means tragic resolve, not pain.

TextENGNew: But what if all of it had been part of a predetermined scenario...?
On that day, that night, at that place, Satoshi-kun...
changeReason: Old translation 'For instance...' is completely wrong content for JPN 悟史くんに、あの日、あの晩に、あの場所で (to Satoshi, on that day, that night, at that place). Text appears misaligned.

TextENGNew: ...What if someone had put the idea into his head to bludgeon his aunt to death......?
...I had no doubt that Satoshi-kun loved his sister and had made his tragic decision to protect Satoko.
But whether he had truly conceived the plan to kill his aunt entirely 'by his own will' remained a mystery.

The Satoshi-kun I knew well... that somewhat optimistic, slightly absent-minded Satoshi-kun... no matter how much it was for his sister's sake... could he really have planned the murder of his aunt on his own...?
changeReason: Original says 'detached'; new says 'absent-minded' -- JPN 抜けた means absent-minded/airheaded, not detached.

TextENGNew: What if, for example, there was a list of planned murders -- and the aunt had been designated as the fourth year's target from the start?
changeReason: New explicitly mentions 'a list of planned murders' (殺人計画リスト) from JPN, omitted in original.

TextENGNew: And someone had skillfully exploited Satoshi-kun's particular circumstances?
changeReason: Original says 'they used Satoshi-kun'; new specifies 'skillfully exploited his particular circumstances.'

TextENGNew: And Satoshi-kun, having become connected to that someone... was eliminated?
Viewed that way, it paralleled the first year's construction manager murder, which was similar in nature.

The ringleader of that crime remained missing to this day, despite the police's exhaustive search.@k In the sense that neither his whereabouts nor his fate -- alive or dead -- was known, it mirrored Satoshi-kun's case.
changeReason: New adds 'despite the police's exhaustive search' and notes the parallel with Satoshi-kun's case.

TextENGNew: The workers who carried out the crime reportedly testified that a minor quarrel had escalated into murder,@k ...but what if that escalation had been 'instigated' by the ringleader?
changeReason: Original says 'fight turned into a slaughter'; new correctly says 'minor quarrel escalated into murder' -- JPN ちょっとした喧嘩.

TextENGNew: What if someone had put the idea into his head -- to carry out the killing on that day, at that time, in that place......?
Yes.@k The first year's incident was... strikingly similar to Satoshi-kun's case in the fourth year.
Then... had Satoshi-kun been dancing in the palm of someone's hand all along...?
Ooishi had posited that Satoshi-kun's disappearance was engineered to sever the connection between a murderer and me.

...But now, reading back over the notes I had written in outline form...@k Satoshi-kun's disappearance did not seem like something improvised on the spot. It felt as though it had been incorporated into a 'schedule' determined long in advance.
I had been an unplanned variable in that 'schedule,' so measures were taken to separate me.
And then, without missing a beat... Satoshi-kun was 'spirited away by the demon,' right on schedule...

At first, I too had believed Satoshi-kun's disappearance was because the Sonozaki family could not publicly tolerate me, a twin of the family's next heir, being in love with Satoshi-kun of the traitorous Houjou family.
changeReason: New adds detail about Shion being 'a twin of the family's next heir' from JPN.

TextENGNew: ...But something was off.@k Something did not sit right.
Satoshi-kun's disappearance had nothing to do with me.
Even if I had never met Satoshi-kun...@k ...he would have killed his aunt in June of Showa 57, and he would have disappeared.
changeReason: New preserves 'Showa 57' from JPN; original converts to '1982.'

TextENGNew: At one point, I had considered taking revenge on my grandmother.
Because I had believed she was the one who tore Satoshi-kun and me apart.
But if our relationship had nothing to do with it...
..........What did that mean?
...Who had made Satoshi-kun disappear...?
To find out, I would have to confront the 'Hinamizawa Village Serial Mysterious Deaths' head-on.
What were these incidents?@k Who was behind them, what was their purpose, and how long would they continue...?
Their intent, their objective.@k And the one responsible.
And... who had taken Satoshi-kun.
If, in the process, I could uncover whether Satoshi-kun was alive or dead...@k .............
...The 'Hinamizawa Village Serial Mysterious Deaths' meant 'Oyashiro-sama's Curse.'@k ...What was Oyashiro-sama's curse...?
What kind of god was Oyashiro-sama, to begin with...?@k What sort of deity was that again...?
What blessings did Oyashiro-sama bestow, and what curses did Oyashiro-sama inflict...?
My thoughts, instead of spilling from my lips, flowed through the tip of my mechanical pencil, spun into words on the page.
Oyashiro-sama is... Oyashiro-sama is...@k My wandering thoughts poured directly onto paper in written form...
That was why.
changeReason: Original says 'I was so absorbed by it...'; new says 'That was why.' -- JPN だから means therefore; completely different meanings.

TextENGNew: When I finally realized that someone had been standing behind me for some time, peering over my shoulder -- and had been doing so for quite a while --@k I could not help letting out a cry of surprise, almost like a scream.
changeReason: Original says 'gasping'; new says 'a cry of surprise, almost like a scream' -- JPN 悲鳴にも似た声.

TextENGNew: An intellectual-looking woman giggled mischievously.
``N-No.@k@vS06/06/150601184. You just surprised me.@k@vS06/06/150601185. ...I am sorry for making such a scene.``
I offered a quick apology, but really, she was the one who had been peeping at me from behind -- she could have apologized too.
But far from apologizing, she stared intently at my face...
``...............Are you Sonozaki Shion-san?``
``...........You know my name, yet we have never met... You must be an acquaintance of my sister's?``
Being a twin is unpleasant at times like these.
The other person already knows who you are, but you have no idea who they are.

``Hee hee, I am sorry.@k@vS06/09/150900006. The Sonozaki-san I know would never set foot in a library.@k@vS06/09/150900007. So I guessed you might be the twin sister I had heard rumors about -- Shion.``
The woman giggled, looking amused.
``As you surmised, I am Sonozaki Shion.@k@vS06/06/150601188. How do you do. Nice to meet you.@k@vS06/06/150601189. Forgive me, but who might you be?``
``Oh, I am sorry. I have not introduced myself yet, have I?``
She seemed proud of her hair, running her fingers through it as she spoke.
``Takano Miyo.@k@vS06/09/150900010. You can call me Miyo, Shion-chan.``
``Thank you for the kind offer, Takano-san.``
changeReason: Original says 'Nice to meet you'; new says 'Thank you for the kind offer' -- JPN お気遣いありがとうございます means the latter.

TextENGNew: I had been feeling an undeniable irritation for some time now, as though I were being subtly looked down upon.
There is nothing I despise more than being condescended to by someone I have just met.
changeReason: Original is vague ('people like that'); new captures specific JPN meaning about being condescended to by a stranger.

TextENGNew: So I closed my notebook and made to stand up, signaling my refusal.
``Oh... are you upset?``
``How perceptive of you. I am deeply moved.@k@vS06/06/150601192. I prefer to think alone, so I will be taking my leave.@k@vS06/06/150601193. Excuse me. Farewell.``
``...What a pity.@k@vS06/09/150900013. I thought we would get along so well.``
``Is that so?@k@vS06/06/150601195. I fail to see why.``
``Because I have finally met a kindred spirit -- a fellow researcher of Oyashiro-sama's curse.@k@vS06/09/150900015. I would think that is quite a rare encounter, would you not agree...?``
My face flushed red.@k ...She had gotten a good look at the contents of my notebook.
``Th-This is just -- my own delusions, fantasies!@k@vS06/06/150601197. It is all nonsense.@k@vS06/06/150601198. Please disregard a child's scribbling!``
``The series of mysterious deaths known as Oyashiro-sama's curse.@k@vS06/09/150900017. Though each incident appears independent, they are all unmistakably driven by a single will.``
``Each year, one person falls victim to Oyashiro-sama's curse and dies, and one person is offered as a sacrifice and vanishes.``
``Wh-...what?@k@vS06/06/150601200. What are you suddenly saying?@k@vS06/06/150601201. One person dies by the curse and... one person... what did you say...?``
Bombarded so abruptly with something so outlandish, I was caught off guard and stammered.
...But I quickly realized she had said something extraordinary.
``You must have noticed that the number of victims in the Hinamizawa serial mysterious deaths is always even, haven't you?@k@vS06/09/150900020. ...Or have you not gotten that far yet?``
The number of victims was always even...??
I tried to organize my confused thoughts by reviewing the incidents so far.
In the first year, the construction site manager died.
That should have been one person.
In the second year, Satoshi-kun's parents... two people.
In the third year... the head priest, one person.@k ...No, if his wife's suicide was also part of the conspiracy, then two...
And in the fourth year, the aunt died... and Satoshi-kun disappeared.
If Satoshi-kun's disappearance was also part of the conspiracy... then the victims were indeed two.
...It was true.@k Except for the first year, the number of victims was always even -- always two.
No, wait.
...I had just concluded myself that the first year's case closely resembled the fourth year's.
The ringleader's whereabouts remained unknown to this day -- which meant that, like Satoshi-kun, the possibility that he too had been 'spirited away by the demon' could not be ruled out.
changeReason: New preserves 'spirited away by the demon' and adds nuance about the possibility not being ruled out.

TextENGNew: ``See?@k@vS06/09/150900022. It makes sense now, doesn't it?``
Like a teacher working through a difficult math problem at the blackboard, Takano-san guided me to the answer.@k ......I could not bring myself to nod, but neither could I find the words to deny it.
``Is that enough of a hint, I wonder...?@k@vS06/09/150900024. I would have loved to discuss this at length, but if you prefer solitude, there is nothing I can do.``
``...Hee hee hee.``
``...I apologize for being rude earlier, Miyo-san.@k@vS06/06/150601204. Please call me Shion.``
``...Pfft... hee hee hee hee...!``
My abrupt about-face seemed to delight her, and she laughed contentedly for a while.
Takano Miyo -- a woman who exuded a faintly suspicious aura.@k Ordinarily, I would not want to get involved with someone so dubious.
changeReason: Original says 'She didn't seem that reliable'; new says 'a woman who exuded a faintly suspicious aura' -- different characterizations.

TextENGNew: But... for someone like me, whose opportunities to meet people were severely limited, this was a rare and precious encounter,@k ...and I could not conceal my interest in what she had to say.
changeReason: Original says 'I don't get to talk to people very much'; new captures the significance of this being a 'rare and precious encounter.'

TextENGNew: ``Let me introduce myself properly.@k@vS06/09/150900028. I am Takano Miyo.@k@vS06/09/150900029. I would be happy if you called me Miyo.``
``Pleased to meet you, Miyo-san.@k@vS06/06/150601206. I am Sonozaki Shion.@k@vS06/06/150601207. Mion's twin sister.@k@vS06/06/150601208. Please call me Shion.``
Miyo-san offered a handshake, and I took her hand as a small gesture of reconciliation.

``Please tell me more about what you said earlier.@k@vS06/06/150601210. You said there are always two victims, correct?@k@vS06/06/150601211. One who dies by the curse, and the other... you said something about a sacrifice?``
``......Shion-chan.@k@vS06/09/150900031. Before we get into that, would you mind telling me why you are interested in this?``
She leaned forward slightly and peered into my eyes.@k ...It was not entirely comfortable.
``......How much do you know about me, Miyo-san?@k@vS06/06/150601213. If you know that much, I imagine you can roughly guess.``
``Hmm...@k@vS06/09/150900033. I only know that you spent several years at a boarding school somewhere far away, and recently returned to Okinomiya...``

``I see.@k@vS06/06/150601215. Then you can probably imagine.@k@vS06/06/150601216. The serial mysterious deaths that occurred while I was away --@k@vS06/06/150601217. I do not know much about them, so I am looking into it.@k@vS06/06/150601218. Just out of curiosity.``
This woman, who had immediately recognized me as Shion rather than Mion...@k ...I had a feeling she knew about Satoshi-kun and me as well.
...Unless she had an intelligence network as formidable as Ooishi's, there was no reason she should know that much -- I could not explain even to myself why I thought so.
It was just something about her eyes -- a knowing quality, as though she already saw through everything...

``Let me ask you as well, Miyo-san.@k@vS06/06/150601220. Earlier, you called me a fellow researcher of Oyashiro-sama's curse.@k@vS06/06/150601221. Why are you researching the curse?@k@vS06/06/150601222. And you stated with certainty that the number of victims in the serial mysterious deaths is always even.``

``It is true that the Hinamizawa serial mysterious deaths are commonly called Oyashiro-sama's curse.@k@vS06/06/150601224. But why would researching the curse lead you to delve into the serial mysterious deaths themselves?``
``Shion-chan, you are asking too many questions at once.@k@vS06/09/150900037. And they are getting tangled -- I cannot tell what you really want to know.``
Ugh...@k I fell silent in spite of myself.
Perhaps I had tried to ask too many things at once and gotten muddled.
``I will answer in order.@k@vS06/09/150900039. ...What I am researching is not the curse per se, but something much broader.``
``The cultural history of Onigafuchi Village.@k@vS06/09/150900041. In plain terms, I am researching the unknown history -- the dark history -- of ancient Hinamizawa.``

``Onigafuchi Village??@k@vS06/06/150601226. Onigafuchi -- you mean that bottomless swamp deep in the village that is said to connect to the realm of the demons?@k@vS06/06/150601227. And what do you mean by unknown history, dark history...?``
changeReason: Original says 'connected to Hell'; new correctly says 'connected to the realm of the demons' -- JPN 鬼の国.

TextENGNew: I fired off my questions in rapid succession.
Every one of them seemed to be exactly what Miyo-san wanted to be asked -- she smiled with evident delight.

``Onigafuchi Village was the name of Hinamizawa before the Meiji Era.@k@vS06/09/150900043. As for the dark history... you must have at least a vague idea, don't you?@k@vS06/09/150900044. The history of the man-eating demons.``
......Ah... the man-eating demons.
That was a folk tale passed down in Hinamizawa.

Long ago, demons came from the demon realm at the bottom of the swamp and battled the villagers,@k but in the end, they decided to live together peacefully.@k And so the villagers, inheriting half human and half demon blood, became semi-divine beings...
changeReason: New includes JPN detail about 'half human and half demon blood' (半人半鬼) and 'semi-divine beings'; original says 'sacred people.'

TextENGNew: The folk tale I had heard somewhere long ago came flooding back to my mind.

``Anyone with ties to Hinamizawa knows this story -- it is common knowledge.@k@vS06/09/150900046. But do you know about the banquets -- where those half-human, half-demon beings descended from the mountains and feasted on the people they had abducted?``
changeReason: New includes full content about banquets where demons 'descended from mountains and feasted on abducted people'; original truncates.

TextENGNew: ``What?@k@vS06/06/150601229. ...Wh-What are you talking about?``
``You are aware that the villagers carry the blood of man-eating demons in their veins -- the demons were man-eaters -- have you never heard that?``
``...........Ah......... well, yes, I suppose there is that version of the story.``
I laughed vaguely and deflected.
Hinamizawa had originally been an insular, impoverished village, but after the war it had gradually expanded its influence, now extending its reach to the town of Okinomiya and beyond.
There were some people who resented or discriminated against those from Hinamizawa, and when they wanted to insult us, they would call us 'man-eating demons.' I was vaguely aware of that.
We younger people did not take such slurs very seriously, but the older generation reacted with extreme hostility to anything of the sort.@k Far too severely for it to ever be laughed off.
So among us, 'man-eating demons' was a taboo phrase, never to be used lightly.
Even for someone in the throes of rebellion like me.
Miyo-san, however, used that forbidden phrase without a hint of hesitation -- as though it did not concern her in the slightest.
``...Are you upset?``
``About what?``
``...I know people hate that phrase in Hinamizawa.@k@vS06/09/150900050. I apologize if I made you feel uncomfortable.``
``The old people wouldn't like it, but I'm fine...@k@vS06/06/150601233. Would you continue?``
As I urged her to go on, Miyo-san smiled reassuringly and resumed talking.
And as I listened to her story about the history of the ancient Onigafuchi and the true meaning of Watanagashi@k ...a strange sound mixed with static started buzzing in my ears.
Gzz... Gzzgzz...
...But from where? I've never heard anything like this before...
Gzz... Gzzgzz...@k Gzz... Gzzgzz...
The sound gradually grew louder and louder,@k scraping the inside of my head and deeply inside my chest.@k It was a slow,@k dull,@k and uncomfortable sound that just droned on.
And as if in response to the sound,@k my heart started throbbing,@k painfully and loudly,@k as though it were a person's anguished cry.
Gzz... Gzzgzz...@k Gzz... Gzzgzz...@k Gzz... Gzzzzzzzzzzzz......

I give Miyo-san vague nods as she continues her story, annoyed at the noisy static ringing in my ears all the while.#k ...That said, her stories of Hinamizawa's past were all nothing short of outrageous.
I was just taken aback.
And when she gathered from my expression how incredulous it all sounded to me...@k She began stringing even more sentences together.
``It's not a hoax. I didn't make any of this up.@k@vS06/09/150900064. If you promise not to tell anyone, I can show you my notes on Watanagashi...``
Miyo-san took out a notebook from her bag and flipped through it.
She appeared to be doing her research thoroughly.
``...Thank you.@k@vS06/06/150601237. I'd love to read it.``
``Sure.@k@vS06/09/150900066. I'd like to keep it confidential...
......Hee hee, but I guess I should share it with my fellow researcher.``
Miyo-san handed me the notebook a bit hesitantly.
It looked like it'd take a whole night to finish reading.
It was densely packed with information.
I saw copies of various documents and quotes in there too. It wasn't just some delusion of hers.
``...You told me@k@vS06/06/150601239. that one person dies because of the curse and another person is sacrificed and disappears, right?``
A person is sacrificed and disappears.
...Satoshi-kun had gone missing.
......Did he become a sacrifice...?@k What is a sacrifice anyway...?
Miyo-san looked pleased to hear my questions again. She took out another notebook and handed it to me.

``I think you'll understand once you read it, but I'll give you a digest version...@k@vS06/09/150900069. Oyashiro-sama's curse is simply Oyashiro-sama's anger.@k@vS06/09/150900070. In other words, the curse is caused by Oyashiro-sama's anger,@k@vS06/09/150900071. do you follow?``
``Yes......@k@vS06/06/150601241. You're saying that Oyashiro-sama was mad at the people who opposed the village during the dam conflict, and that's why the curse occurred.``
``That's right.``
``Oyashiro-sama's anger has to be pacified.@k@vS06/09/150900074. If not, something even worse will occur.
So, all the successive priests at the Furude Shrine had to perform a sacrifice every time the curse struck, in order to pacify Oyashiro-sama.``
``So...... each year on the day of Watanagashi, one person dies because of the curse,@k@vS06/06/150601243. and another one is sacrificed to pacify Oyashiro-sama's anger?!``
B-But... only one of them actually died.@k And there's no public record of any other casualties in these incidents...!
``Naturally, there's no body.@k@vS06/09/150900078. The ritual of sacrifice requires the offering be sunk into Onigafuchi Swamp.``
Miyo-san instantly responded to dismiss my faint hopes.@k I couldn't help but shudder at the mocking smile that appeared on her face.
...It's true. They say that nothing sunk into the bottomless swamp of Onigafuchi may ever rise again.@k It would be the perfect place to hide a body...
``So... one of the victims is always thrown into the swamp every year...?``
Does that mean Satoshi-kun is also at the bottom of that dark green swamp...?

``I don't know...@k@vS06/09/150900082. I'm not sure that they really throw them into the swamp.@k@vS06/09/150900083. But those who make these incidents happen must be eliminating one person based on that legend.``
Each year a traitor to the village dies on the day of Watanagashi because of Oyashiro-sama's curse.
And another person is sacrificed in order to pacify Oyashiro-sama's anger.
...That person's body will never be found, because he or she is supposedly thrown into the bottomless swamp.
``Yes, you get it...@k@vS06/09/150900085. The series of mysterious deaths always involves both the curse and the sacrifice.``
``It's a system that allows two traitors to die every year.``
``What I'm telling you now probably isn't sufficient,@k@vS06/09/150900088. so it might be hard to grasp.@k@vS06/09/150900089. Instead, I want you to take the time to read my notes.``
``You'll see that what I'm telling you isn't far from the truth at all...``
I heard a man's voice just then.
A middle-aged man with a hat was waving at Miyo-san from the entrance.

``...Sorry, I think I have to go.@k@vS06/09/150900092. I wanted to talk to you more,@k@vS06/09/150900093. but... I guess we'll have another chance to chat.@k@vS06/09/150900094. I'll leave those notes to you.
I'm looking forward to hearing your opinions.``
Miyo-san wrapped up the conversation and headed to where the man was waiting for her.
She left her old notebooks in my hands.
Miyo-san waved at me as she left the library with her companion.
...I took a seat again and opened what she'd given me...
What bugged me was the series of mysterious deaths,@k also known as the curse of Oyashiro-sama.
Maybe I'd be able to get closer to the truth by reading these notebooks...
If I studied them carefully, I might be able to find out who was really behind the incidents.
Why did Satoshi-kun have to disappear...? What was he involved in? How did that happen...?
Maybe Takano's notes would bring me the answer.
......Research notes - Miyo Takano
I started flipping the pages, this time making sure that no one was watching over my shoulder......
It was time for the library to close. I had to leave.
I bought some food on my way home.
I shut my door and locked it right away.
Then, I heard the door of the neighboring apartment open.
Kasai lived next door.
...Nobody else lived on this floor of the nearly empty apartment building anyway.
I clearly heard the door open and close.
Kasai must've noticed that I came home, and was coming over because he wanted to talk to me.
I unlocked the door and shouted out to him.
``Is that you, Kasai?@k@vS06/06/150601247_2. I'll leave the door unlocked for you.``
``I'm not Kasai-san, Shion.``
That voice...@k I twitched.
``Can I come in...?``
The door slowly opened.
......My alter ego Mion Sonozaki appeared.
She was holding a box of cake...
While smiling timidly.
``......Are you going to keep standing there until I escort you?@k@vS06/06/150601251. Come in.@k@vS06/06/150601252. Sorry this room doesn't look very cute.``
``...It looks comfortable.``
Sis looked a bit tense when entering my room. It was her first time here.
``How's life...?``

``My new school is as boring as I thought it would be.@k@vS06/06/150601254. I usually go, but I skip classes when I don't feel up to it.@k@vS06/06/150601255. I couldn't do that when I was going to St. Lucia's.``
``Ahahahahaha.@k@vS06/03/150300232. So St. Lucia's was really tough, huh?``
``Hah, you should go there sometime.``
``Ahaha, sorry.@k@vS06/03/150300234. I brought cake. Let's eat.``
Mion opened the box. I saw two cheesecakes there.
We have the same taste in food,@k so we don't have to fight if we get two of the same thing.
I chatted with her over the cheesecake.
I talked to her about school and whatnot.
``If you need furniture, tell me.@k@vS06/03/150300236. I might be able to get some for you.``
``Hmm.@k@vS06/06/150601258. I finally got used to this room, but I might move out soon. So furniture won't be necessary. Thanks, though.``
``Dad wants me to come back home...@k@vS06/06/150601260. I don't want to live with him, but he sounds desperate.``
``Ahahahaha.@k@vS06/03/150300238. He'd be pleased if you came home. He looked lonely while you were gone.``
``Hah, you can only say that because you don't have to live with him.``
``Ahahahahaha.``
Come to think of it, this was the first time I'd talked to Sis since I was allowed to live as Shion Sonozaki.
She didn't look like how she was at the Sonozaki house that day.
The successor of the Sonozaki family and my twin sister are two different people.
The demon lives inside the leader of the Sonozakis.
Our bodies are exactly the same, except for the tattoo of a demon on her back.
Mion's destiny as the successor was set in stone when she got that tattoo.
.........It's pretty simple.@k The Mion from that night was different from the Mion before me.
When I was forced to tear off my fingernails, I'd momentarily despised her.
But if I were the next leader of the family...@k I'd have done the same.
``......Shion...@k@vS06/03/150300241. how are... your fingernails?``

``The wounds have closed.@k@vS06/06/150601263. I don't bandage them anymore because it looks too conspicuous...@k@vS06/06/150601264. but my nails still look ugly, so I don't really like showing them to people...``
I sneered at her, displaying my incomplete fingernails.
Mion grew quiet and looked down.

``......You don't have to apologize.@k@vS06/06/150601266. You did what you had to do.@k@vS06/06/150601267. You just played the role of the successor.@k@vS06/06/150601268. I'm not holding any grudges.``

``Okay!@k@vS06/06/150601270. Apology accepted.@k@vS06/06/150601271. But!@k@vS06/06/150601272. If you apologize one more time, that'll be canceled!@k@vS06/06/150601273. I won't forgive you for the rest of my life!``
``Once you start apologizing, you never stop,@k@vS06/06/150601275. so I'm cutting you off.``
``...Will you really forgive me...?@k@vS06/03/150300246. About Satoshi-kun, as well...?``
My heart ached when she mentioned his name.
...It felt just like the ache of an old wound.
``...I figured you loved him.``
``No wonder. I shouted it out loud in front of all of you.@k@vS06/06/150601277. I'm not embarrassed anymore.@k@vS06/06/150601278. Ahahahaha.``
``Granny truly believes you've atoned.@k@vS06/03/150300249. She thinks you've done all you needed to do,@k@vS06/03/150300250. so the matter is finished.``
``......That's good.@k@vS06/06/150601280. My pain won't go to waste, then.``
``I wonder where... Satoshi-kun disappeared to, though...``
I started choking when I heard her say that.
I'd just said it to her myself...
I said that I'd forgiven her completely...@k but that promise instantly evaporated...
Just because of what she said, I felt like I'd turned into a completely different person...
...What did you just say, Mion...?
Did you say 'I wonder where Satoshi-kun disappeared to'...?
Who else could know other than you and your folks?
What did you say...?@k Where did he go......?
I felt my eyeballs reddening in anger. It felt as though they were about to pop.
I choked on a burning sensation in my throat.
Mion's expression rapidly changed.
It looked like she noticed the devil's mask on my face.
We were one and the same.
...We could understand each other's thoughts without voicing them.
So showing our expressions was like unveiling everything in our hearts to the other.
Even though I'd told her not to apologize anymore, Mion apologized.
...I felt like strangling her...
I was going to make her confess where she'd hid Satoshi-kun...
If she told me she threw him into the Onigafuchi Swamp, I'd strangle her to death right then and there......!!!
``I really don't know... what happened to Satoshi-kun...``
That's a lie.
``...It's true...@k@vS06/03/150300256. Granny doesn't know anything either...@k@vS06/03/150300257. Please believe me...!``
